refinement_sections.content: The client wants to know if their wellness programs help reduce stress for employees. We need to look at survey data from different departments, but we don't know how stress levels differ between these departments before the programs started.

The survey data is not clear about how many people from each department were surveyed or what their stress levels were before the programs began. We need this information to understand the differences.

We need to choose a statistical test to compare stress levels before and after the wellness programs. A t-test might work if we only have two groups, but if there are more, we might need ANOVA. I need to check if the data is normal or not before deciding.

I assume that the stress levels can be measured numerically and that we can use these numbers in our tests. I also assume that the survey responses are honest and that there are enough responses from each department.
After doing the test, I will explain if the wellness programs made a difference in stress levels. If the p-value is low, we can say the programs helped. If it’s high, we might say they didn’t help.
